name: Cerro Becerrero Trail
name_original: Castillo de Estepa — ida y vuelta desde la población de Estepa
description: Hike the moderate Cerro Becerrero Trail, a 6.1-mile route with 1241 feet of elevation gain, featuring Estepa Castle and Cerro Becerrero.
region_intro: Hiking around Estepa offers diverse landscapes, from mountainous regions to riverine environments. The area is characterized by prominent features such as the Sierra de Gilena and Cerro Becerrero, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. Trails often traverse rolling hills and offer views of historical landmarks like Estepa Castle. The region's natural features include the tranquil Genil River Meander, contributing to a range of hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Estepa The most popular hiking route is Mirador de la Acebuchosa — circular por la Sierra de Gilena, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 42 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views across the Sierra de Gilena. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cerro Becerrero Trail, a moderate 6.1 miles (9.8 km) path. This trail incorporates views of Estepa Castle and the surrounding hills. Local hikers also love the Sierra de Becerrero Circular Trail, a 10.1 miles (16.2 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Becerrero, often completed in about 4 hours 44 minutes. Hiking around Estepa is defined by mountainous regions, riverine landscapes, and historical landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, with 9 easy and 14 moderate routes available. The routes in Estepa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 26 reviews. More than 125 hikers have used komoot to explore Estepa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Estepa?
A: Estepa offers a diverse network of over 20 hiking trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, with 9 easy and 14 moderate options available for exploration.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for hiking in Estepa?
A: The hiking trails around Estepa are primarily categorized as easy and moderate. There are 9 easy routes, perfect for a relaxed stroll, and 14 moderate routes that offer a bit more challenge with varied terrain and elevation changes.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Estepa?
A: Yes, Estepa features several circular hiking routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1250518515/mirador-de-la-acebuchosa-circular-por-la-sierra-de-gilena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Acebuchosa — circular por la Sierra de Gilena&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1250404247/sierra-de-becerrero-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Becerrero Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; offers a longer, immersive experience.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while hiking in Estepa?
A: Hikers in Estepa can enjoy diverse natural landscapes. You'll encounter mountainous regions like the Sierra de Gilena and Cerro Becerrero, and serene riverine environments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Genil River Meander&lt;/a&gt;. The area also boasts unique geological formations like the ancient quarry of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4083606" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coto de las Canteras (Osuna)&lt;/a&gt;, often referred to as the 'Petra of Andalusia'.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks visible from the trails in Estepa?
A: Yes, several trails offer views of historical landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009686967/cerro-becerrero-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Becerrero Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, provides scenic vistas of Estepa Castle. Additionally, the region is rich in history, with sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4083325" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calle San Pedro Palaces&lt;/a&gt; in the vicinity.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Estepa?
A: The hiking trails in Estepa are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 26 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the scenic views across the Sierras, and the blend of natural beauty with historical points of interest.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Estepa?
A: Estepa offers 9 easy routes that are generally suitable for families looking for a less strenuous outdoor experience. These paths typically feature gentler inclines and shorter distances, allowing for an enjoyable outing with children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Estepa?
A: While specific regulations can vary, many trails in natural areas around Estepa are generally dog-friendly, especially if your dog is kept on a leash. It's always recommended to check local signage at trailheads for any specific restrictions or requirements.

Q: Are there trails with good viewpoints in Estepa?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its scenic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1250518515/mirador-de-la-acebuchosa-circular-por-la-sierra-de-gilena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Acebuchosa — circular por la Sierra de Gilena&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views across the Sierra de Gilena. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5394288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Malpasillo Dam and Genil River&lt;/a&gt; highlight is another excellent spot for expansive scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Estepa?
A: The best time for hiking in Estepa is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, the weather is milder and more comfortable for outdoor activities, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential chill of winter.

Q: How long do hikes in Estepa typically take to complete?
A: The duration of hikes in Estepa varies depending on the route and your pace. Moderate trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1250518515/mirador-de-la-acebuchosa-circular-por-la-sierra-de-gilena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Acebuchosa — circular por la Sierra de Gilena&lt;/a&gt; can take around 2 hours 42 minutes, while longer routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1250404247/sierra-de-becerrero-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Becerrero Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; might take closer to 4 hours 44 minutes.

Q: Are there any trails that follow a river or offer water features?
A: Yes, the region includes riverine landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009919293/ribera-del-rio-de-las-yeguas-circular-desde-casariche" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribera del Rio de las Yeguas — circular desde Casariche&lt;/a&gt; trail follows the banks of the River Yeguas. You can also explore the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Genil River Meander&lt;/a&gt;, a notable natural feature in the area.
tips: Esteva is a town known for having an important manufacturing industry of two typical Christmas sweets, mantecado and polvorón, especially in Spain. In the town, however, there is its castle, towering over the rest and where you cannot leave the town without having visited the castle.
The hill of Estepa repeaters, wide road in good condition, little traffic. Nice views of Estepa (for example, Cerro de San Cristóbal and Torre de la Victoria).

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike the Cerro Becerrero Trail?
faqs.answer: The Cerro Becerrero Trail is a moderate hike that generally takes around 3 hours to complete. This estimate is for the full 9.8 km route.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as a moderate hike. It involves an elevation gain of approximately 378 meters, making it suitable for hikers with a reasonable level of fitness.

faqs.question: Is the Cerro Becerrero Trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The Cerro Becerrero Trail is designed as a round trip, meaning it is a loop route that brings you back to your starting point.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route is described as a 'round trip from the town of Estepa'. While specific parking details for the trailhead are not available, it's generally advisable to look for public parking options within Estepa when starting this hike.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the Cerro Becerrero Trail, you'll encounter two notable highlights: the historic Estepa Castle and the Cerro Becerrero summit itself, offering potential views of the surrounding area.

faqs.question: Is the Cerro Becerrero Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding specific dog restrictions for the Cerro Becerrero Trail is not readily available. It's always best to check local regulations or signage at the trailhead regarding dogs, especially concerning leash requirements.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit this trail?
faqs.answer: As specific seasonal information for the Cerro Becerrero Trail is not detailed, general advice for hiking in this region would be to consider spring and autumn for milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring cooler, potentially wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that a permit or entrance fee is required to hike the Cerro Becerrero Trail. However, it's always good practice to check for any local updates before your visit.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of other official trails. Approximately 66% of the route follows the [CIMA SV05] Cerro Becerrero trail, and smaller portions also utilize parts of Calle Antonio Machado.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families with children or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating and approximately 378 meters of elevation gain over 9.8 km, the trail might be challenging for very young children or absolute beginners. Families with older, active children or beginners with some hiking experience might find it enjoyable.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hike?
faqs.answer: While specific terrain details for the Cerro Becerrero Trail are not available, a moderate hike of this length and elevation typically involves varied paths, which could include dirt tracks, rocky sections, and some inclines and declines as you ascend to the summit and visit the castle.
